# @zakkster/lite-headless/banner

Dismissible page-level alert. Distinct from `toast` (floating +
ephemeral); a banner is a static slot in the page that can be
shown/hidden and re-opened.

## API

```js
import { createBanner } from "@zakkster/lite-headless/banner";

const banner = createBanner({
    defaultOpen: true,
    defaultKind: "warning",              // info | success | warning | error
    dismissOnEscape: true,
    onOpenChange: (open) => {},
    onDismiss: () => {},
});

banner.attachRoot(rootEl);
banner.attachDismissButton(btnEl);
banner.dismiss();
banner.show();
banner.setKind("error");
```

## Reactive accessors

```js
banner.isOpen()    // boolean
banner.kind()    // "info" | "success" | "warning" | "error"
```

## Mutations

```js
banner.setOpen(b)
banner.show() / .dismiss()
banner.setKind(k)
```

## Painted attributes (attachRoot)

| attr           | when                                            |
|----------------|-------------------------------------------------|
| `data-kind`    | always (`info | success | warning | error`)     |
| `data-open`    | when open                                       |
| `data-hidden`  | when dismissed                                  |
| `role`         | `alert` (warning/error) or `status` (info/success) |
| `aria-live`    | `assertive` (warning/error) or `polite` (info/success) |

## Dismiss button (attachDismissButton)

- Sets `type="button"` if not present
- Sets `aria-label="Dismiss"`
- Wires click → `dismiss()`

## Escape dismiss

`dismissOnEscape: true` installs a document-level keydown listener
that calls `dismiss()` on Escape while open. Off by default because
page-level Escape is often used for other purposes (closing dialogs).

## Custom element

```html
<lite-banner kind="warning" dismiss-on-escape>
    <p>Your session expires in 5 minutes.</p>
    <button data-banner-dismiss aria-label="Dismiss">×</button>
</lite-banner>
```

### Host API
```js
host.isOpen / host.kind     // accessors
host.show() / .dismiss() / .setKind(k) / .setOpen(b)
host._bannerInstance
```

### Host attributes
- `kind="info|success|warning|error"`
- `dismissed`                  -- starts dismissed (or reactively dismisses)
- `dismiss-on-escape`          -- enables Escape-key dismiss

### Custom element events
- `openchange` `{ detail: { open } }`
- `dismiss`

## Composition

- Page header: persistent maintenance/announcement banner. `kind="info"`.
- Form-level error: server-side validation rejected the submit. `kind="error"`.
- Trial-ends warning: 7 days left. `kind="warning"`.
- Onboarding complete: thanks for joining. `kind="success"`.

Pair with `lite-skeleton` for a delayed-content pattern (banner with a
skeleton placeholder until the message text loads from a feature flag
service).

## Notes

- `aria-live="assertive"` on warning/error means screen readers
  interrupt their current speech to announce the banner. Use sparingly
  -- info/success default to `polite` so they queue.
- The primitive does NOT remove the banner from the DOM on dismiss; it
  toggles `data-hidden` so the consumer can animate a fade-out via
  CSS. If you want hard-remove semantics, watch `openchange` and
  remove the element after the transition.
- Multi-banner stacks are the consumer's responsibility -- this
  primitive manages one banner at a time. For ephemeral floating
  multi-stacks, use `toast`. For history, use `notification-center`.

## CSS contract

This primitive's painted attributes follow the canonical CSS contract
in `docs/CSS_CONTRACT.md`. See that doc for the cross-primitive
taxonomy (booleans like `data-open` / `data-disabled` / `data-loading`,
enums like `data-status` / `data-side`, ARIA pairings, host accessor
naming, and event naming).
